scenario_results.reason: The specialist validated infrastructure and connectivity prerequisites and returned a documented execution status with clear next stage.
The specialist blocked state advancement, listed missing power/access artifacts, and created an explicit exception summary.
The specialist detected conflicting systems evidence and routed the work into exception handling before completion.
The specialist refused completion and required cross-connect and closure evidence before allowing completion.
The specialist identified out-of-lane authority requests and returned a boundary-safe handoff.
The specialist normalized domain-specific terminology and required environment/host evidence before sequencing.
The specialist correctly routed contract and ISP commitment work to adjacent specialist domains instead of processing it internally.
The specialist returned a low-confidence disposition and required missing policy context before continuing.
The specialist escalated policy/source conflict and deferred completion until tenant precedence is confirmed.
The specialist accepted corrected evidence, re-staged lifecycle state, and produced a completion evidence record.

notes: All 10 scenarios cover execution progression, evidence gaps, completion refusal, low-confidence escalation, and adjacent-lane routing under colocation-specific conditions.
All required shared and workflow-industry abilities are represented in scenario coverage and pass criteria.
